retrieval_events.query: Blaise: Well, I guess I try to find little ways to cope, but it often feels like a drop in the bucket. Sometimes I’ll go for a walk or listen to music to clear my head, but those moments are usually short-lived. I often find myself just pushing through, thinking that if I can just get through the next task or conversation, things will calm down. But then, it’s like I’m just delaying the inevitable. I know I should probably be more proactive about finding ways to manage my stress, but it’s hard to prioritize that when I feel like I’m constantly in crisis mode. I guess I just keep hoping that once the wedding is over or once this project at work wraps up, I’ll finally have a chance to breathe. But then, there’s always something else waiting around the corner. It’s frustrating because I want to feel more in control, but it often feels like I’m just reacting to everything instead of taking charge of my own well-being.

retrieval_events.query: Blaise: You know, when I think about it, I can trace some of these beliefs back to my childhood. My parents always emphasized the importance of family loyalty and being there for one another, which I get—it’s a nice value to have. But it often felt like there was this unspoken rule that you had to put everyone else first, no matter what. I remember times when I wanted to do something for myself, but it was always about making sure everyone else was happy or taken care of first. It’s like I learned that my worth was tied to how much I could do for others. I guess that’s why I feel so guilty about wanting to take time for myself now. It’s hard to shake off that conditioning, especially when it feels like I’m betraying those family values if I prioritize my own needs. It’s frustrating because I want to break free from that cycle, but it feels like I’m constantly battling against these ingrained beliefs.

It’s frustrating because I know I should be able to find a balance, but it often feels like I’m stuck in this cycle of obligation. I guess I just keep hoping that one day I’ll find a way to break free from that guilt and feel okay about putting myself first, but it’s hard to see how that could happen when I’ve been conditioned this way for so long.
